fixed.title: Basics quiz
fixed.description: The following covers basic MaaFW knowledge. You must answer all questions correctly to proceed. If you are new to MaaFW, use the docs or AI to help — it will greatly help with future project maintenance. If you already know this material, you'll pass quickly!
fixed.errorWithScore: Score {{correct}}/{{total}}. Please fix the highlighted questions and resubmit.
fixed.errorAllWrong: Some answers are incorrect. Please get all questions right before submitting.

random.title: Tips quiz
random.description: The following covers common tips. A score of 60% or higher is required to pass.
random.errorWithScore: Score {{correct}}/{{total}}. Accuracy below 60%. Please fix the highlighted questions and resubmit.
random.errorInsufficient: Accuracy below 60%. Please review and resubmit.

intro.title: Welcome to MaaPipelineEditor
intro.description: MaaPipelineEditor (MPE) is a visual Pipeline editor for MaaFramework (MaaFW), helping you edit task flows graphically.
intro.warningDescription: MPE is an auxiliary tool for MaaFW and cannot replace learning MaaFW itself. Make sure you understand MaaFW basics (such as Node, Task, Pipeline, etc.) before using this editor.
intro.docsHint: If you are not familiar with MaaFramework yet, please read the official docs first:

certificate.title: Congratulations — you passed!
certificate.subTitle: You have the basics of MaaFW. Time taken: {{time}}. Welcome to MaaPipelineEditor!

terms.tool_nature: I understand that MaaPipelineEditor (MPE) is an auxiliary visual editor for MaaFramework and cannot replace learning and understanding MaaFW itself.
terms.no_warranty: I understand this tool is an open-source community project provided "as is" without any express or implied warranty. I am solely responsible for any consequences of using this tool.
terms.data_local: I understand that all data in this tool is stored locally in the browser and is not uploaded to any server. Clearing browser data may cause work to be lost, and I will back up when necessary. (Configuration can be exported from the settings panel.)
terms.project_allow: For projects I will develop or assist with using MPE, I am the primary decision-maker, or I have consulted the main maintainer and received permission to use MPE for development or assistance. (Some projects do not use MPE as the primary dev tool — project architecture may not fit, or may produce redundant content. When contributing to other projects, follow their conventions and tool recommendations.)

q0.question: What is the relationship between MaaPipelineEditor (MPE) and MaaFramework (MaaFW)?

options.0: MPE replaces MaaFW entirely, similar to a higher-level wrapper
options.1: MPE is an optional MaaFW component that replaces hand-written Pipeline JSON, similar to a module/library
options.2: MPE is an independent tool in the MaaFW ecosystem that assists MaaFW project development, similar to an IDE
options.3: MPE and MaaFW are unrelated — two completely independent projects

q1.question: MPE can fully replace learning MaaFW; you can use it without knowing MaaFW first.

q2.question: You can create a brand-new blank project directly in MPE, or reuse the resource subdirectory of MPE Desktop as the project directory

q3.question: When starting a MaaFW project, it is recommended to use the official template ([MaaPracticeBoilerplate](https://github.com/MaaXYZ/MaaPracticeBoilerplate)) or the [create-maa-project](https://github.com/Windsland52/create-maa-project) scaffold rather than creating folder structure manually.

q4.question: What is the MaaFW (MaaFramework) project template repository URL?

q5.question: Which button must you use when creating a project from the template repository?

q6.question: Which subfolders should a standard MaaFW Resource folder contain?

q7.question: When developing your own MaaFW project, you do not need to download OCR models separately — the project template or MPE handles it.

q8.question: Which files must be in the ./model/ocr folder under the resource directory?

q9.question: In MaaFW, what is the basic format of a Pipeline?

q10.question: In MaaFW, what does Task refer to?

options.3: A logical sequence of nodes connected in a certain order

q11.question: When a node's next list has multiple child nodes, how does MaaFW handle it?

options.0: Randomly picks one child to execute
options.2: Loops through them in order; on first match, enters that branch and continues
options.3: Waits until all children are recognized successfully before executing

q12.question: When a node with the [JumpBack] attribute in the next list is matched and executed successfully, what happens?

options.1: Returns to the parent immediately without running that node's subsequent chain
options.2: Tries to recognize and run its subsequent chain, then returns to the parent

q13.question: For a non-entry node, in which cases does execution enter its on_error list?

options.0: The previous node failed to match this node's recognition and timed out
options.1: This node failed to match recognition of nodes in its own next list and timed out
options.3: A matched next node's action execution failed

q14.question: For an entry node (Entry), in which cases does execution enter its on_error list?

options.0: At task start, matching the entry node's own recognition failed and timed out
options.1: The entry node failed to match recognition of nodes in its own next list and timed out
options.2: The entry node's action execution failed
options.3: A matched next node's action execution failed

q15.question: Which of the following will terminate a MaaFW task flow?

options.0: After action execution, the current node's next list is empty and this sub-chain was not entered via jumpback
options.1: After action execution, next list recognition timed out and on_error is not configured
options.2: The current node's action failed and on_error is not configured
options.3: The current node executes StopTask action or post_stop() is called directly

q16.question: In a MaaFW project, what is interface.json for? (MPE only handles Pipeline, not interface)

options.0: Defines recognition algorithms for Pipeline nodes
options.1: Declares project structure so a generic UI can load and run the project

q17.question: How to fill in interface.json (PI protocol) is documented in section 3.2 of the MaaFW manual.

q18.question: Which tool provides JSON content checking for MaaFW projects?

q19.question: Which project is specified as a best-practice reference in the MaaFW manual?

q0.question: After next list recognition times out, which list is entered?

q1.question: Changing a node's timeout field cannot fix recognition match timeout for the current node.

q2.question: Which statement about a node's on_error list is incorrect?

options.0: Recognition timeout for nodes pointed to by the node's own next enters on_error
options.1: The node's own action failure enters on_error
options.2: After entering on_error, nodes in the list are not recognized
options.3: The node's own recognition miss and timeout does not enter on_error

q3.question: on_error is entered only when action execution fails.

q4.question: ROI (Region of Interest) defines the boundary region for image recognition.

q5.question: Targets outside the ROI (Region of Interest) will not be recognized.

q6.question: In MaaFW, what does Entry refer to?

options.1: The first Node in a Task

q7.question: Template images for TemplateMatch should be scaled to what resolution by default?

q8.question: The MaaFW pipeline folder recursively reads all JSON files with no limit on count or nesting depth.

q9.question: When using TemplateMatch for recognition, you need to understand YOLO algorithm internals.

q10.question: Which files must the OCR model folder contain?

q11.question: After development, which method does the community recommend for multi-platform packaging?

options.0: Manually bundle resources and generic UI into a zip locally
options.1: Cross-compile the generic UI on your machine
options.3: Tag a version with git and push to GitHub
